description_long: This object is a robust, metal hand tool with a flat, elongated handle and a distinctive forked end. The handle is straight and tapers slightly towards a circular opening at the opposite end, which may serve as a secondary functional feature. The forked end is broad and features two prongs with a central notch, designed for prying or pulling tasks, such as removing nails or lifting objects. The entire tool is constructed from a dark, metallic material, giving it a solid and durable appearance suitable for heavy-duty urban or construction use. The surface is smooth with a matte finish, and there are no visible rubberized or ergonomic enhancements on the handle. No branding or logos are visible on any part of the tool.
description: A sturdy, dark metal hand tool with a straight handle and a wide, forked prying end, featuring a circular opening at the opposite end. The surface is smooth and matte, with no visible branding or grip enhancements.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the tool, highlighting the straight handle and the wide, forked prying end.
description_view_1: End-on view from the circular opening, emphasizing the handle's thickness and the symmetry of the forked prying end.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the first but with the circular opening on the left and the prying end on the right.
description_view_3: View from the prying end, showing the two prongs and central notch, as well as the handle's width and taper.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, flat metal handle with a circular opening at one end; no insulation or ergonomic features.
attribute_jaw_design: Wide, forked prying end with two flat prongs and a central notch; designed for prying or pulling rather than gripping.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: No pivot mechanism; the tool is a single, solid piece with no moving parts.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal surface with no visible grip pattern or texturing.
